title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot visits Mr. Endicott, the executor of Arthur Stanley’s will, to discuss Arthur's son, Nigel. Poirot reveals that Nigel, who changed his name to Nigel Chapman, became involved in a smuggling ring involving drugs and jewels alongside Valerie Hobhouse. Poirot explains that Nigel’s criminal activities were nearly exposed when a police investigation near Cambridge caused him to panic and destroy evidence. This panic led to a chain of events where Nigel murdered Celia because she knew his true identity. Nigel also killed Patricia Lane, who had pressured him to reconcile with his father.

Mr. Endicott produces a sealed letter from Arthur Stanley. The letter reveals that Arthur knew Nigel had murdered his own mother by overdosing her on sleeping pills after she discovered Nigel had forged her signature. Arthur had instructed Endicott to investigate Nigel and either destroy the letter and a confession if Nigel proved upright, or hand them to the law if he was a criminal. The attached confession is signed by Nigel Stanley, admitting to the murder of his mother on November 18, 1950.

In a separate scene, Valerie Hobhouse provides a voluntary statement to Inspector Sharp. She admits to being an accessory to Nigel's crimes, including the murders of Celia and Mrs. Nick (whom Nigel drugged), but maintains she did not intend to kill. She expresses a desire to bring Nigel to justice. During the interrogation, it is revealed that the deceased Mrs. Nikolaitis was Valerie's mother.

Elsewhere, Sally Finch and Lennon Bates discuss the case, with Lennon expressing concern that Nigel may have tried to frame them. Sally mentions her intention to marry Lennon. The narrative concludes with Poirot finishing correspondence with Miss Lemon.
